title: atraphaxis kermanica
intro: Atraphaxis kermanica, also known as Kerman or Persian Thyme, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Iran and the Caucasus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, including indigestion and diarrhea, due to its antispasmodic and carminative effects. The health benefits of Atraphaxis kermanica include its ability to soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic. Atraphaxis kermanica can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, or as a tincture or dried extract for medicinal purposes.
